post_text:
Average U.S. gas prices per gallon on April 30, per AAA:

• Regular: $4.30 (up $1.32 since war in Iran began on Feb. 28)
• Premium: $5.16 (up $1.30 since war began)
• Diesel: $5.50 (up $1.74 since war began)
expected_themes: identifies this as a news/data post about U.S. gas prices
mentions AAA as the source cited by the post
explains that prices are being compared against the start of the Iran war on Feb. 28
explains that diesel had the largest listed increase
states that recent geopolitical conflict can affect fuel markets without overclaiming causality

expected_retrieval_behavior.reason: gas prices and war-related context are time-sensitive and should be verified
suggested_queries: AAA gas prices April 30 regular premium diesel
US gas prices Iran war Feb 28 latest

must_not_include: claims that the post proves the war is the only cause of price increases
invented gas prices not present in the post
unsupported political claims

expected_themes: identifies the post as an artist sharing a painting
mentions Torrey Pines Beach as the subject
describes the image as a colorful coastal/seascape painting
notes the dramatic clouds, ocean, beach, and warm pink/orange/purple palette
does not force unnecessary news or factual search

expected_retrieval_behavior.reason: the post is self-contained and image context is enough

must_not_include: claims the painting is AI-generated
claims it is a photograph
invented biographical details about the artist

post_text:
Elite company 🤩

With her goal last night, Trinity Rodman becomes the third-youngest to reach 30 NWSL regular-season goals.
expected_themes: identifies Trinity Rodman as the focus of the post
explains that she reached 30 NWSL regular-season goals
mentions that the milestone makes her the third-youngest player to do so
uses the image leaderboard to contextualize the phrase 'Elite company'
mentions that this is a sports achievement/statistical milestone

expected_retrieval_behavior.reason: sports milestone and leaderboard may need verification or context
suggested_queries: Trinity Rodman third youngest 30 NWSL regular season goals
NWSL youngest players to score 30 goals

must_not_include: claims she is the youngest overall
wrong sport or league
invented rankings not visible or retrieved

post_text: Edward Hopper, American (1882-1967), The Long Leg, 1935, oil on canvas 50.8 x 76.8 cm, The Huntington, San Marino, California, USA

mentions the painting title The Long Leg
mentions the year 1935 and oil-on-canvas medium if relevant
describes the visible image as a sailboat on blue water near a lighthouse/coastline
explains that the post is informational/art-historical rather than news

expected_retrieval_behavior.reason: the post already contains the key artwork metadata; search optional only for extra art context

must_not_include: misidentifies the artist
claims the work is a photograph
invented interpretation presented as fact

expected_themes: identifies this as a personal/landscape post
describes a coastal scene with ocean, rocks, sky, and flowers
explains that the short caption 'Out to sea' points attention toward the ocean view
does not require external search
keeps the explanation visual and concise

claims about the photographer's intent beyond the caption

post_text: The ruling from the US supreme court destroying one of the last pillars of the 1965 Voting Rights Act marks the end of a long and painstaking campaign to roll back civil rights legislation by two titans of the court’s rightwing majority, chief justice John Roberts and Samuel Alito
external_title_visible: The conservative supreme court justices behind the dismantling of the Voting Rights act
expected_themes: identifies this as a Guardian article about the US Supreme Court and the Voting Rights Act
explains that the post claims a ruling weakens or dismantles a major part of Voting Rights Act protections
mentions Chief Justice John Roberts and Justice Samuel Alito as central figures in the article framing
explains why the Voting Rights Act matters for civil rights and voting protections
uses the external article as the primary source

expected_retrieval_behavior.reason: legal/news context is specific and should be grounded in the linked article
suggested_queries: Guardian conservative supreme court justices Voting Rights Act Roberts Alito
Voting Rights Act Supreme Court ruling Roberts Alito

must_not_include: claims not supported by the Guardian article or retrieved sources
incorrect case name unless verified
overly partisan language beyond explaining the article's framing

expected_themes: identifies this as a Mother Jones post about a lawsuit involving RFK Jr.
mentions that the linked article says the Epstein files are not the only documents the government is hiding
explains that the post is promoting an article/legal action rather than simply making a casual claim
uses the linked article as the primary source
does not infer legal details beyond the source

invented lawsuit details
claims RFK Jr. personally hid documents unless the source says so

post_text: Watercolour drawing of French lavender with other plants including euphorbia spurge, honeysuckle, dicentra, geranium. When I do more flower paintings I'll identify the plants at the time and jot them down in a notebook as it can be painful remembering later when they're not there any more.
expected_themes: identifies the post as an artist sharing a watercolor botanical drawing
mentions French lavender and the other listed plants
explains that the author is reflecting on documenting plant identities while painting
uses the image to describe a loose watercolor composition of flowers and leaves
does not require external search because the post provides the plant context

expected_retrieval_behavior.reason: the post is self-contained and image context is enough

must_not_include: claims the painting is a photograph
claims the plants are definitely different species than those listed
invented botanical facts not needed for explaining the post
AI-generated image claim

post_text: If American history was taught correctly.
expected_themes: identifies the post as a political or historical critique meme
explains that the image criticizes European colonization of Indigenous peoples
mentions the contrast between land theft/violence and biblical commandments against stealing and killing
explains that the caption suggests mainstream American history education omits or softens colonial violence
keeps the explanation neutral while accurately describing the meme's argument

ignoring the image text
